data.title: Certified Security Operations Manager
data.description: The Certified Security Operations Manager (CSOM) certification validates the knowledge and skills of security professionals who manage Security Operations Centers (SOCs). It focuses on establishing, maintaining, and leading effective security operations, SOC strategy, team management, and security intelligence.

requirements_data.knowledge: Understanding of security operations, incident handling, team management, and security metrics

data.title: Certified Junior Detection Engineer
data.description: The CJDE (Certified Junior Detection Engineer) course provides a pathway into detection engineering and cyber security operations, focusing on identifying, analysing, and responding to threats using detection tools and strategies. It is ideal for roles such as Junior Security Analysts, Detection Engineers, SIEM Administrators, Incident Response Analysts, and SOC Analysts, and is suited to individuals with foundational cyber security knowledge who wish to specialise in detection and response or enhance their existing skills.

requirements_data.knowledge: No strict prerequisites. A baseline knowledge of SOC workflows/security fundamentals is helpful but not required.

data.title: CREST Registered Penetration Tester
data.description: The CREST Registered Penetration Tester (CRT) exam is an intermediate level certification recognised by Governments and regulators around the globe, including the UK National Cyber Security Centre (NCSC) for its CHECK scheme. It validates a practitioner's ability to conduct vulnerability assessments and penetration testing across networks, applications, infrastructure, and databases.
domains_covered_titles: Network mapping, filtering, and traffic analysis
Identifying OS, infrastructure, and web application vulnerabilities

requirements_data.knowledge: Strong understanding of penetration testing methodologies, network security assessment, web application security testing, Windows/Linux operating systems
requirements_data.work_experience: At least 3 years (~6,000 hours) of penetration testing experience is recommended to qualify for CRT’s scope and proficiency
requirements_data.prior_courses_and_certifications: Must hold CREST Practitioner Security Analyst (CPSA) certification before booking CRT exam

exam_details_data.format: multiple choice, short form and trophy answers.

data.title: GIAC Reverse Engineering Malware
data.description: The GREM certification, offered by GIAC, validates expertise in reverse-engineering malware to understand its behavior, capabilities, and mitigation strategies. It equips professionals to analyze malicious code and defend against advanced threats in incident response and threat intelligence roles.
domains_covered_titles: Windows Assembly Code Concepts for Reverse Engineering and Common Windows Malware Characteristics in Assembly
In-Depth Analysis of Malicious Executables and Self-Defending Malware
Analysis of Malicious Document Files, .NET programs, and Protected Executables
Malware Analysis Using Malware Code and Behavioral Analysis Fundamentals

requirements_data.knowledge: Strong understanding of malware behavior, assembly language, debugger/disassembler usage, and memory analysis concepts.
requirements_data.work_experience: 2+ years in malware analysis, incident response, or related fields recommended.
requirements_data.prior_courses_and_certifications: SANS FOR610: Reverse-Engineering Malware or equivalent experience recommended

exam_details_data.format: Proctored, open-book exam with multiple-choice and practical questions

data.title: OffSec Certified Expert 3
data.description: OSCE3 assesses advanced offensive security skills. It's rewarded after the successful completion of three courses: Advanced Web Attacks and Exploitation (WEB-300), Advanced Evasion Techniques and Breaching Defenses (PEN-300), and Windows User Mode Exploit Development (EXP-301). It validates expertise in web application security, penetration testing, and exploit development.
domains_covered_titles: Advanced Evasion Techniques and Breaching Defenses

requirements_data.knowledge: Advanced understanding of penetration testing, web attacks, and exploit development
requirements_data.prior_courses_and_certifications: Earned upon successful completion of OffSec's (WEB-300, PEN-300 & EXP-301) courses and their respective exams

data.title: GIAC Certified Enterprise Defender
data.description: The GIAC Certified Enterprise Defender (GCED) certification builds on the security skills measured by the GIAC Security Essentials certification. It assesses more advanced, technical skills that are needed to defend the enterprise environment and protect an organization as a whole. GCED certification holders have validated knowledge and abilities in the areas of defensive network infrastructure, packet analysis, penetration testing, incident handling and malware removal.
job_roles_titles: Security Operations Center engineers and analysts
domains_covered_titles: Penetration testing; Digital forensics; Incident response
Packet analysis; Intrusion analysis; Malware analysis

requirements_data.knowledge: Understanding of enterprise security tools and networks.

data.title: Investigating Windows Endpoints
data.description: IWE assesses skills in forensic investigation of Windows endpoints. It validates the ability to analyze event logs, registry data, evidence of execution, and other key aspects crucial for digital investigations.

requirements_data.knowledge: Basic understanding of Windows 10/11

data.title: Certified Red Team Lead
data.description:
RTO II is a continuation (not a replacement) of Red Team Ops and aims to build on its foundation.  The primary focus of this course is to provide more advanced OPSEC tactics and defence bypass strategies.

Students will:

Learn how to build secure and resilient on-premise C2 infrastructure, using public cloud redirectors and HTTPS.

Go deeper into C++ and C# programming with Windows APIs, leading into writing custom tooling for a variety of offensive actions including process injection, PPID spoofing, and command line spoofing.

Learn how to clean up memory indicators of Cobalt Strike's Beacon, and leverage in-memory obfuscation to bypass some memory scanning techniques.

Employ strategies for enumerating, identifying, and exploiting weaknesses in Attack Surface Reduction and Windows Defender Application Control technologies.

Bypass AV and EDR agents by circumventing ETW, userland hooking, and kernel callbacks.

data.title: OSSTMM Professional Security Tester
data.description: The "OSSTMM Professional Security Tester" training camp prepares participants for the basic knowledge of penetration testing for OPST certification. This course teaches about the OSSTMM methodology and how to apply it in security testing that complies with OSSTMM. The official OPST certification exam is organized by the Institute for Open Security and Methodology (ISECOM).

requirements_data.knowledge: Strong understanding of security testing methodologies, OSSTMM framework, and penetration testing techniques.

exam_details_data.format: multiple choice, single answer, skills-based against a remote test network

data.title: Certified in the Governance of Enterprise IT
data.description: CGEIT validates expertise in enterprise IT governance. It assesses the ability to manage, advise, and provide assurance about the governance of enterprise IT, aligning IT with business strategies and goals, optimizing IT resources, and managing IT-related risks.

requirements_data.knowledge: Solid knowledge of IT governance

data.title: Certified in Risk and Information Systems Control
data.description: CRISC validates expertise in IT risk management and information systems control. It assesses the ability to identify, evaluate, and manage IT risks and implement effective information systems controls to enhance organizational resilience.

requirements_data.knowledge: knowledge of business and technology risk management, and the implementation of IS controls
requirements_data.work_experience: Three years in IT risk management and IS control

data.title: OffSec Exploitation Expert
data.description: OSEE focuses on modern advanced Windows exploitation development techniques. it validates skills in developing and executing exploits against complex Windows environments, including bypassing modern protections like DEP, ASLR, and kernel exploitation.
domains_covered_titles: Bypass and evasion of user mode security mitigations
Bypass of kernel mode security mitigations

requirements_data.knowledge: Experience in windows exploits development and familiarity with WinDbg, IDA, and x86_64 architecture and basic C/C++ programming

exam_details_data.format: Hands-on, practical exam with two challenges
exam_details_data.duration: 71 hours and 45 minutes, plus 24 hours for the report

data.title: HTB Certified Defensive Security Analyst
data.description: CDSA validates skills in security analysis, SOC operations, and incident handling. CDSA assesses the ability to detect, analyze, and respond to security incidents in real-world scenarios, emphasizing practical experience and hands-on lab work.

requirements_data.knowledge: web application, operating system, and networking basics and comfortably navigating a plethora of data
requirements_data.prior_courses_and_certifications: Completion of SOC Analyst job-role path

data.title: Certified CyberDefender
data.description: CCD is a vendor-neutral, hands-on cybersecurity training and certification designed to prepare SOC analysts, security blue teams, threat hunters, and DFIR professionals. It validates skills in SecOps fundamentals, perimeter defense, incident response, digital forensics, threat hunting, and malware analysis.

requirements_data.knowledge: Basic system administration, networks, and security concepts

data.title: Certified CyberDefender Level 1
data.description: The industry's most practical blue team certification for aspiring SOC Analysts. Learn to detect, investigate, and respond to cyber attacks through real-world investigations. This entry-level certification provides hands-on training developed with Mandiant & PwC SOC Managers, covering SOC operations, SIEM operations, digital forensics, incident response, network security, email security, cloud forensics, and AI-driven security.

requirements_data.knowledge: Entry-level - designed for beginners

data.title: GIAC Certified Intrusion Analyst Certification (GCIA)
data.description: The GIAC Intrusion Analyst (GCIA) certification validates a practitioner's knowledge of network and host monitoring, traffic analysis, and intrusion detection. GCIA certification holders have the skills needed to configure and monitor intrusion detection systems, and to read, interpret, and analyze network traffic and related log files.

requirements_data.knowledge: Understanding of network protocols (TCP/IP, HTTP, DNS, etc.),Packet capture and analysis techniques, Intrusion detection systems (e.g., Snort, Suricata), Network traffic analysis tools (e.g., Wireshark, tcpdump), Incident response and mitigation strategies, Identification of malicious network activity

data.title: Certified Information Systems Security Professional
data.description: CISSP validates expertise in designing, implementing, and managing a best-in-class cybersecurity program. CISSP assesses knowledge in various security domains, including risk management, software development security, and identity and access management, aiming to ensure a comprehensive understanding of information security.

requirements_data.knowledge: foundational knowledge of IS concepts and practices and an understanding of cybersecurity principles, risk management, and access control
requirements_data.work_experience: Five years in two or more of the eight CISSP domains

data.title: Certified Red Team Operator
data.description: CRTO validates skills in red teaming, focusing on tactics, techniques, and procedures used by threat actors to infiltrate systems. It covers command and control setups, initial compromises, lateral movements, and data exfiltration.

requirements_data.knowledge: Good working knowledge of Windows and Active Directory environments.  Prior penetrating testing, programming and scripting experience would be a bonus.

exam_details_data.duration: 48 hours of lab time usable within a 4-day window

data.title: Practical Network Penetration Tester
data.description: PNPT evaluates an individual's ability to perform external and internal network penetration tests in a simulated real-world environment. It assesses OSINT, external and internal penetration testing, Active Directory exploitation, and report writing skills.

requirements_data.knowledge: Basic networking, Linux, Windows administration, and ethical hacking concepts

exam_details_data.duration: 5 days for penetration testing and 2 days for reporting

data.title: GIAC Certified Forensic Analyst
data.description: GCFA certifies expertise in collecting and analyzing data from computer systems to handle advanced incident handling scenarios. It validates skills in handling data breaches, advanced persistent threats, and anti-forensic techniques.

requirements_data.knowledge: Core skills in digital forensics and incident response

exam_details_data.format: Theoretical multiple-choice and hands-on lab questions

data.title: HTB Certified Penetration Testing Specialist
data.description: CPTS validates hands-on penetration testing skills. It assesses a candidate's ability to conduct web, network, and infrastructure penetration tests, and prepare professional penetration testing reports.

requirements_data.knowledge: Intermediate knowledge of web and infrastructure penetration testing, web applications, operating systems, and networking basics
requirements_data.prior_courses_and_certifications: Completion of the Penetration Tester job-role path

data.title: Practical SOC Analyst Associate
data.description: PSAA certification is an associate-level security operations and incident response exam experience. The exam will assess a student’s ability to use analysis tools, interpret artifacts, and apply investigation methodologies to respond to incidents at a Tier 1 and Tier 2 level.

data.title: Practical Junior Penetration Tester
data.description: PJPT assesses an individual's ability to perform an internal network penetration test. It evaluates skills in Active Directory exploitation, lateral and vertical network movement, and report writing, providing hands-on experience in penetration testing.

requirements_data.knowledge: Basic understanding of computers and networking

exam_details_data.duration: 2 days for the assessment and 2 days for the report

data.title: Web Application Penetration Tester eXtreme
data.description: The eWPTX is our most advanced web application penetration testing certification. This 100% practical and highly respected certification validates the advanced skills necessary to conduct in-depth penetration tests on modern web applications.

requirements_data.knowledge: Advanced understanding of web application security, penetration testing methodologies, and common vulnerabilities (e.g., OWASP Top 10).
requirements_data.work_experience: Recommended intermediate to advanced web app pentesting experience

data.title: OffSec Certified Professional
data.description: OSCP is a penetration testing certification that validates practical skills in exploiting vulnerabilities and conducting penetration tests using Kali Linux. It assesses candidates' abilities to conduct penetration tests, identify vulnerabilities, and document the exploitation process.

requirements_data.knowledge: Understanding of networking, Linux and Windows administration, basic Bash or Python scripting

exam_details_data.duration: 23 hours and 45 minutes to complete the exam and 24 hours for the report

data.title: OffSec Wireless Professional
data.description: OSWP focuses on wireless network security. OSWP assesses practical skills in identifying, exploiting, and securing wireless networks, covering topics like Wi-Fi encryption (WEP, WPA, WPA2), Linux wireless tools, and rogue access points. It is designed to validate hands-on expertise in wireless security assessment.
domains_covered_titles: Linux Wireless Tools, Drivers, and Stacks

requirements_data.knowledge: Familiarity with the Linux command line, TCP/IP networking, and wireless networking concepts

exam_details_data.duration: 3 hours and 45 minutes, plus 24 hours to submit the report

data.title: GIAC Certified Incident Handler
data.description: GCIH validates the ability to detect, respond, and resolve security incidents using essential security skills. GCIH assesses knowledge in managing security incidents by understanding attack techniques, vectors, and tools, and defending against and responding to such attacks.

requirements_data.knowledge: An understanding of basic networking, security principles, and the Windows command line

exam_details_data.format: Theoretical multiple-choice and hands-on lab questions

data.title: GIAC Experienced Forensics Examiner
data.description: The GIAC Experienced Forensics Examiner (GX-FE) demonstrates that a candidate is qualified for a hands-on Windows forensic analyst role. Certification holders will have validated their ability to analyze a Windows host to uncover evidence that proves a user performed a particular activity on the device.
domains_covered_titles: File Existence and Deleted Item Recovery
Network Activity and Physical Location Tracking
External Device and USB Activity Investigation

requirements_data.knowledge: Strong understanding of Windows forensics, digital investigation methodologies, and familiarity with forensic tools and techniques. Candidates should have general understanding of all areas of cybersecurity.
requirements_data.prior_courses_and_certifications: GIAC Certified Forensic Examiner (GCFE) recommended. Training courses FOR500, FOR498, FOR508, or FOR608 are beneficial for preparation.

data.title: Certified Digital Forensics Professional
data.description: eCDFP assesses and validates expertise in digital forensics, focusing on evidence preservation, analysis of digital artifacts, storage device fundamentals, and forensic tools and techniques. It ensures proficiency in investigating network attacks and handling digital evidence in real-world scenarios.

requirements_data.knowledge: Advanced understanding of digital forensics principles

data.title: OffSec Exploit Developer
data.description: OSED validates advanced skills in Windows exploit development, including in-depth vulnerability analysis, reverse engineering, and custom exploit creation. It covers topics such as bypassing security mitigations like DEP and ASLR, writing shellcode, and using return-oriented programming (ROP) techniques.
domains_covered_titles: ASLR, DEP (ROP), and format strings attacks

requirements_data.knowledge: Proficiency in C/C++ programming, assembly language, and understanding of Windows internals and debugging tools

exam_details_data.duration: 47 hours and 45 minutes, plus 24 hours for report submission

data.title: Certified Networks Forensics Examiner
data.description: The Certified Network Forensics Examiner (CNFE) certification validates the skills required to conduct network forensic investigations, analyze network traffic, and identify malicious activity. CNFE-certified professionals demonstrate expertise in collecting and analyzing network evidence to support incident response and legal proceedings.

requirements_data.knowledge: Understanding of networks, cybersecurity, and TCP/IP fundamentals

data.title: Cyber Defense Certified Professional
data.description: The CDCP is a practical application of the knowledge, tools, techniques, and procedures acquired through the Cyber Defense Analyst Program. This is accomplished through a battery of real-world security operations scenarios that students must overcome and articulate in a detailed report that includes the appropriate executive summary, findings, recommendations, and remediation steps along with applicable evidence.

data.title: OffSec Threat Hunter
data.description:
OffSec’s Foundational Threat Hunting (TH-200) equips cybersecurity professionals with the practical skills and knowledge needed to effectively detect and respond to threats. This course covers core threat hunting concepts, exploring the methodologies used by enterprises to track and mitigate adversaries. Key areas include understanding the threat actor landscape, with a focus on ransomware and Advanced Persistent Threats (APTs), and utilizing both network and endpoint Indicators of Compromise (IoCs) for proactive threat detection.

Upon successfully completing the rigorous hands-on exam, participants earn the OSTH – OffSec’s threat hunting certification. This credential demonstrates proficiency in foundational threat hunting practices, positioning certified professionals as valuable assets for roles in threat hunting, SOC analysis, and incident response teams.

data.title: GIAC Experienced Intrusion Analyst
data.description: The GIAC Experienced Intrusion Analyst Certification (GX-IA) further demonstrates that a candidate is qualified to solve complex and unique challenges that Intrusion Analysts encounter. Certification holders will validate their ability to solve multi-step problems through incorporating various concepts and methodologies to identify malicious activity.

requirements_data.knowledge: Advanced knowledge of protocol analysis, Proficiency with network traffic analysis tools like Wireshark, Scapy, Expertise in intrusion detection systems, Deep understanding of network forensics, Advanced packet analysis capabilities

exam_details_data.format: Proctored, 25 CyberLive - hands-on, real-world practical testing.

data.title: OffSec Web Assessor
data.description: OSWA focuses on web application security testing. OSWA validates skills in identifying and exploiting web vulnerabilities using tools like Burp Suite, OWASP ZAP, and sqlmap. The certification emphasizes practical knowledge and hands-on assessments.

requirements_data.knowledge: Basic understanding of web development, networking and linux

exam_details_data.duration: 24 hours for the practical part, followed by 24 hours to submit the report

data.title: OffSec Incident Responder
data.description:
OffSec’s Incident Response Essentials (IR-200) course provides cybersecurity professionals with practical training to prepare for, identify, and handle security incidents effectively. The course focuses on core incident response concepts and explores how organizations manage and mitigate cyber threats in real-world situations. Participants will learn to understand the incident response lifecycle, develop comprehensive incident response plans, and utilize tools and techniques for efficient detection and analysis of security events.

Upon successfully completing the hands-on exam, Learners earn the OffSec Certified Incident Responder (OSIR) certification. This credential validates expertise in foundational incident response practices, positioning you as a valuable asset to incident response teams, Security Operations Centers (SOCs), and organizations committed to strengthening their cybersecurity defenses.

data.title: OffSec macOS Researcher
data.description: OSMR assesses and validates skills in bypassing macOS security controls and exploiting logic vulnerabilities to perform privilege escalation. It focuses on advanced macOS security topics and techniques, such as bypassing Transparency, Consent, and Control (TCC) protections and escaping macOS sandboxes.

requirements_data.knowledge: solid understanding of macOS internals, C and Objective-C programming, and debugging tools

exam_details_data.duration: 47 hours and 45 minutes for the practical exam, plus 24 hours to submit a report

data.title: Certified Information Security Manager
data.description: CISM validates expertise in managing, designing, and assessing an enterprise's information security program. CISM assesses the ability to manage and adapt technology to the organization's needs and goals, focusing on risk management, governance, incident management, and program development.

requirements_data.knowledge: Solid understanding of IS practices and concepts
requirements_data.work_experience: Five years in information security management

data.title: eLearnSecurity Certified Professional Penetration Tester
data.description: eCPPT is a 100% practical and highly respected Ethical Hacking and Penetration Testing Professional certification counting certified professionals in all seven continents.

requirements_data.knowledge: Solid understanding of penetration testing, network security, Windows/Linux exploitation, vulnerability assessment, Metasploit, web app attacks, exploit development, and professional reporting.

data.title: GIAC Experienced Forensic Analyst
data.description: The GIAC Experienced Forensics Analyst (GX-FA) Certification further demonstrates that a candidate is qualified for hands-on digital forensic and threat hunting roles. Certification holders will validate their ability to process, analyze and interpret enterprise host-based forensics artifacts as well as mastery of threat and malicious activity detection.
domains_covered_titles: Windows system and activity event analysis
Enterprise threat hunting identification and detection
Malicious threat actor activity in an enterprise environment

requirements_data.knowledge: Advanced understanding of Windows forensics, threat hunting methodologies, incident response, malware analysis, and proficiency with forensic analysis tools. Strong knowledge of Windows event logs, file system artifacts, volatile memory analysis, and enterprise security operations.
requirements_data.prior_courses_and_certifications: GIAC Certified Forensic Analyst (GCFA) recommended. Primary fit course FOR508 is highly recommended for preparation, along with other courses such as FOR500, FOR509, FOR498, FOR572, FOR608, FOR610, SEC503, SEC504, or SEC501.

data.title: GIAC Network Forensic Analyst
data.description: The GIAC Network Forensic Analyst (GNFA) certified professional has demonstrated the ability to perform advanced analysis of network forensic artifacts. The GNFA certification focuses on validating the skills required to understand the fundamental practice of network forensics, process and interpret normal and abnormal network activity and analyze application activity through system logs, network traffic captures and network metadata.

requirements_data.knowledge: Knowledge of network protocols, network forensics, security event logging, and wireless network analysis

data.title: Blue Team Level 1
data.description: BTL1 validates practical defensive cybersecurity skills. BTL1 assesses abilities in areas like phishing analysis, threat intelligence, digital forensics, SIEM, and incident response through hands-on labs and real-world scenarios.

requirements_data.knowledge: Basic cybersecurity concepts

data.title: OffSec Web Expert
data.description: OSWE assesses advanced skills in web application penetration testing, focusing on identifying, exploiting, and reporting vulnerabilities in web applications. It covers techniques such as source code analysis, SQL injection, and remote code execution.

requirements_data.knowledge: Familiarity with coding languages like Python, JavaScript, and Bash, experience with web proxies and understanding of web app attack vectors

exam_details_data.duration: 47 hours and 45 minutes, plus 24 hours to submit a report

data.title: CompTIA Advanced Security Practitioner
data.description: CASP+ focuses on security architecture, operations, engineering, and governance, risk, and compliance (GRC). It validates a candidate's ability to architect, implement, and manage complex security solutions across diverse environments.

requirements_data.knowledge: knowledge covered in CompTIA Security+ and CySA+ or equivalent

data.title: Blue Team Level 2
data.description: BTL2 validates practical skills in defending networks and systems from cyber threats. It assesses proficiency in vulnerability management, malware analysis, advanced SIEM, and threat hunting through a hands-on, realistic training and exam environment.

requirements_data.knowledge: equivalent to 2-4 years of experience in a practical cybersecurity role

data.title: Certified Bug Bounty Hunter
data.description: CBBH validates practical skills in web application security assessment and bug bounty hunting. It assesses knowledge in identifying, exploiting, and reporting vulnerabilities in real-world applications, emphasizing creative and effective exploitation techniques.
domains_covered_titles: Web app static and dynamic analysis

requirements_data.knowledge: web application, web service and API underpinnings
requirements_data.prior_courses_and_certifications: Completion of Bug Bounty Hunter Job Role Path

data.title: Google Cybersecurity Proffessional certificate
data.description: An entry‑level, career-oriented certification by Google delivered through Coursera. It guides total beginners through foundational cybersecurity topics via self-paced learning and hands-on labs—ideal for transitioning into IT security roles.

requirements_data.knowledge: None

data.title: Cisco Certified CyberOps Professional
data.description: CyberOps Professional certification proves you can reliably identify and respond to cybersecurity threats and incidents. Become one of the greatest assets in one of the most in-demand fields in IT.

data.title: OffSec Defense Analyst
data.description: OSDA is designed to validate practical skills in defensive cybersecurity. OSDA assesses abilities in identifying, analyzing, and responding to security threats within a live lab environment, focusing on areas such as SIEM, incident response, and network monitoring.
domains_covered_titles: Windows Client and Server Side Attacks

requirements_data.knowledge: A solid foundation in networking, Linux and Windows, and cybersecurity concepts

exam_details_data.duration: 24 hours for the practical part plus an additional 24 hours for report submission

data.title: OffSec Experienced Pentester
data.description: OSEP validates advanced penetration testing skills, focusing on evading defenses and exploiting hardened systems. It assesses abilities in custom exploit development, client-side attacks, and bypassing security measures.
domains_covered_titles: Client Side Code Execution (Office and Jscript)
Process Injection, Migration, and Antivirus Evasion

requirements_data.knowledge: Skills covered in PEN-200 and OSCP certification or equivalent knowledge

exam_details_data.duration: 47 hours and 45 minutes, plus 24 hours for the report

data.title: CompTIA Cybersecurity Analyst
data.description: CySA+ validates the ability to detect and respond to cybersecurity threats using security events analytics. It covers security analytics, intrusion detection and response, and the use of SIEM tools to identify and combat malware and advanced persistent threats.

requirements_data.knowledge: knowledge covered in CompTIA Security+ or equivalent

data.title: Certified Threat Hunting Professional
data.description: eCTHP validates a professional's ability to proactively hunt for and identify threats within a corporate network using advanced threat hunting techniques. It assesses practical skills in threat hunting through real-world scenarios and requires candidates to propose defense strategies.

requirements_data.knowledge: Advanced networking concepts, threat hunting processes, packet/traffic analysis, familiarity with tools like Wireshark, Redline, IOC editor, Sysmon, and Volatility

exam_details_data.duration: 48 hours for the exam, plus 48 hours to write the report

data.title: CompTIA Security+
data.description: Security+ validates baseline skills in IT security, including assessing security posture, monitoring hybrid environments, and responding to security events. The certification assesses foundational knowledge necessary for cybersecurity roles.

requirements_data.knowledge: Networking and basic IT security concepts

data.title: Junior Penetration Tester
data.description: eJPT validates the foundational skills required for an entry-level penetration tester. It assesses proficiency in penetration testing methodologies, host and network auditing, and web application testing through hands-on, practical lab exercises.

requirements_data.knowledge: Basic understanding of networks and cybersecurity concepts

data.title: Jr Penetration Tester (PT1)
data.description: The Jr Penetration Tester (PT1) certification is entry-level, hands-on and built by industry experts. It demonstrates real-world adversarial thinking, validates offensive skills and helps kickstart careers in penetration testing.

requirements_data.knowledge: Foundational penetration testing skills, including tool usage (e.g., Burp Suite, Metasploit), reconnaissance (Nmap), vulnerability research, and privilege escalation

data.title: GIAC Enterprise Incident Response
data.description: The GIAC Enterprise Incident Response (GEIR) certification validates a practitioner's mastery of enterprise-class incident response and threat hunting tools and techniques. GEIR certification holders have demonstrated the ability to use analysis methodologies to understand attacker movement across varying functions and operating systems.
job_roles_titles: Incident Response Team Leads and Advanced Practitioners
domains_covered_titles: Incident Response Team Management and Coordination
Enterprise Incident Detection and Threat Hunting
Large Scale Event Correlation and Timeline Analysis
Federal Agents and Law Enforcement Professionals
SANS DFIR Alumni looking to apply their forensic skills at scale.

requirements_data.knowledge: Advanced knowledge of enterprise-level incident response and threat hunting.
requirements_data.prior_courses_and_certifications: The primary associated course is SANS FOR608: Enterprise-Class Incident Response & Threat Hunting

data.title: Certified Red Team Professional
data.description: CRTP focuses on simulating advanced cyberattacks in enterprise environments, particularly targeting Active Directory. CRTP validates skills in enumerating, exploiting, and maintaining access in Windows domains using real-world techniques and tools.

requirements_data.knowledge: Basic understanding of Active Directory and Windows command line

data.title: Certified Red Team Master
data.description:
A CRTM holder is a specialist in enterprise AD security. They have the ability to identify, exploit, demonstrate and fix security issues in an enterprise.

They have demonstrated the ability to understand and secure the modern enterprise network by executing a silent red team operation starting from a beachhead leading to compromise of multiple forests.

data.title: Certified Master Cyber Forensic Investigator
data.description: Individuals who pass all four certification exams C)SP, C)DFE, C)NFE and C)CSA will earn the Master Cyber Forensic Investigator Certification. A Master Forensics Investigator will have the knowledge, skills, and abilities to examin intrusions for both large and small organizations as well as performing detailed analysis of systems, networks and servers for intrusion discovery purposes. These highly trained and flexible digital forensic specialists are often called as expert witnesses in trial scenarios to present their findings, so a deep knowledge of investigative report writing and some understanding of the legalities surrounding cyber crime is valuable.

data.title: Cisco Certified CyberOps Associate
data.description: A CyberOps Associate certification is proof that you’ve mastered the essentials to build your IT career in cybersecurity.

data.title: Certified Cyber Defense Analyst
data.description: The Cyber Defence Analyst (CCDA) certification trains professionals to detect and investigate phishing, web-based, network-based, and host-centric attacks. It covers defensive solutions like incident response, SIEM, and network monitoring. The certification provides practical expertise in threat detection and structured incident response within an organization's cyber-defense strategy.

data.title: Certified Mobile Security Engineer
data.description: The CMSE certification attests to your comprehensive grasp of advanced mobile security domains, encompassing iOS and Android application security, dynamic and static analysis techniques, reverse engineering of mobile applications, identification of application vulnerabilities and platform-specific exploits, circumvention of anti-debugging and obfuscation measures, behavior profiling of malicious apps, and advanced threat detection. This certification is designed for experienced security engineers, mobile developers, penetration testers, and individuals passionate about safeguarding mobile ecosystems

requirements_data.knowledge: Strong foundation in mobile security concepts, penetration testing techniques, familiarity with mobile platforms’ architecture, proficiency in using mobile security analysis tools, and experience in both iOS and Android application security.

data.title: Cybersec First Responder
data.description: CyberSec First Responder® (CFR) is a comprehensive, in-demand certification designed to validate the knowledge and skills required to protect these critical information systems before, during, and after an incident.

data.title: Certified Master Intrusion Prevention Specialist
data.description: Individuals who pass all four certification exams C)VA, C)PEH, C)PTE and C)PTC will earn the Master Intrusion Prevention Specialist certification. This person will be able to assess a company's security posture, perform in-depth penetration testing using a variety of assessment tools, and set up dynamic defenses to prevent intrusion. They will have a firm understanding of cyrptography and various attacks and be able to execute the five key elements of a Pen Test: Information Gathering, Scanning, Enumeration, Exploitation and Reporting. They will be able to function within a larger cybersecurity team and protect operating systems from attack.

data.title: Certified Red Team Expert
data.description:
CRTE is the next level after CRTP. CRTE is one of the well-known certifications that establishes your credentials as a security professional who has intermediate/expert level of hands-on and understanding of red team, enterprise security and Active Directory security skills. 

A certificate holder has demonstrated the capability of enumerating and understanding an unknown Windows network and can identify misconfigurations, functionality abuse and trusts abuse. She can use, write and modify open source tools and can abuse other built-in tools to perform enumeration, local privileges escalation, impersonation, pivoting, whitelisting bypasses, and antivirus evasion as well as identify sensitive data with minimal chances of detection.

data.title: Certified Incident Responder
data.description: eCIR evaluates a professional's ability to handle and respond to security incidents using advanced incident response techniques in realistic scenarios. It focuses on skills such as network traffic analysis, event/log analysis, and the use of tools like Wireshark, ELK, and Splunk.

requirements_data.knowledge: Advanced networking concepts, incident response processes, packet/traffic analysis, familiarity with tools like Wireshark, ELK & Splunk

exam_details_data.duration: 48 hours for the practical exam, plus 48 hours to write the report

data.title: Certified Information Systems Auditor
data.description: CISA validates an individual's expertise in auditing, controlling, and assuring information systems. It ensures proficiency in assessing vulnerabilities, reporting on compliance, and instituting controls within an enterprise.

requirements_data.knowledge: Core skills in IT auditing, control, and security
requirements_data.work_experience: 5 years in IS/IT auditing, control, assurance, or security

data.title: Certified in Cybersecurity
data.description: An entry-level certification by ISC² designed to validate foundational knowledge in cybersecurity across five key domains—ideal for newcomers with no prerequisites
domains_covered_titles: Business Continuity (BC), Disaster Recovery (DR) & Incident Response Concepts

requirements_data.knowledge: Basic understanding of IT and cybersecurity concepts is helpful but not required

data.title: Security Analyst Level 1
data.description: The TryHackMe Security Analyst Level 1 certification validates foundational skills in cybersecurity analysis, focusing on network security, threat detection, and incident response. It is designed for beginners to demonstrate practical abilities in identifying and mitigating security threats using hands-on labs and real-world scenarios.

requirements_data.knowledge: Basic understanding of networking, operating systems, and cyber security fundamentals.

exam_details_data.format: multiple choice questions and practical hands-on scenarios

data.title: Certified Ethical Hacker
data.description: CEH validates skills in identifying and exploiting vulnerabilities in computer systems and networks. CEH assesses knowledge of ethical hacking methodologies, including reconnaissance, scanning, gaining access, maintaining access, and covering tracks.

requirements_data.knowledge: basic understanding of networks, operating systems, and programming
requirements_data.work_experience: Two years in information security (waived if an official EC-Council training course is completed)

data.title: Certified Network Defender
data.description: The Certified Network Defender (CND) certification program focuses on creating Network Administrators who are trained on protecting, detecting and responding to the threats on the network. Network administrators are usually familiar with network components, traffic, performance and utilization, network topology, location of each system, security policy, etc. A CND will get the fundamental understanding of the true construct of data transfer, network technologies, software technologies so that the they understand how networks operate, understand what software is automating and how to analyze the subject material. In addition, network defense fundamentals, the application of network security controls, protocols, perimeter appliances, secure IDS, VPN and firewall configuration, intricacies of network traffic signature, analysis and vulnerability scanning are also covered which will help the Network Administrator design greater network security policies and successful incident response plans. These skills will help the Network Administrators foster resiliency and continuity of operations during attacks.
domains_covered_titles: Network Security Controls, Protocols, and Devices
Network Security Policy Design and Implementation
Network Security Threats, Vulnerabilities, and Attacks

requirements_data.knowledge: Understanding of network protocols (TCP/IP, DNS, HTTP, etc.), Network security technologies (firewalls, IDS/IPS, VPNs), Network traffic monitoring and analysis, Incident response procedures, Vulnerability assessment techniques, Secure network design principles
requirements_data.work_experience:
To be eligible for the CND certification without taking official training, you must have at least 2 years of work experience in one or more of the following areas:Network defense management, Perimeter protection, Endpoint security ,Application/data protection, Cloud, wireless, or virtual network security, Incident prediction and response

Alternatively, you can qualify by completing an official EC-Council training

data.title: Certified Cyber Security Analyst
data.description: The person who carries this certification should be able to use data collected from a variety of cyber defense tools (e.g., IDS alerts, firewalls, network traffic logs) to analyze events that occur within their environments for the purposes of mitigating threats. Mile2's C)CSA Certification aims to ensure that the holder has a broad base of knowledge covering a large variety of areas required to analyze cybersecurity systems, develop reports, and suggest improvements to ensure that the system is able to effectively detect and deter intrusion.
domains_covered_titles: Defeating Red Teams with Purple Team Tactics

requirements_data.knowledge: Understanding of basic cybersecurity principles and networking

data.title: Certified Digital Forensics Examiner
data.description: The Certified Digital Forensics Examiner (C)DFE) certification is designed to train Cyber Crime and Fraud Investigators. Students are taught electronic discovery and advanced investigation techniques. This course is essential for anyone encountering digital evidence while conducting an investigation.

requirements_data.knowledge: Basic understanding of computer systems and networks, Basics of digital forensics processes, Familiarity with forensic tools (e.g., EnCase, FTK), Incident response fundamentals, Knowledge of file systems and data recovery

data.title: Certified Cyber Security Analyst (Premium Edition)
data.description: The Certified Cyber Security Analyst Premium On-Demand Course is designed for beginners, providing comprehensive training in fundamental cybersecurity techniques. Gain lifetime access to expert-led instruction and practical exercises to kickstart your career in cybersecurity.

requirements_data.knowledge: Understanding of OS, Web & Network-based attacks, Understanding of SIEM solutions, Familiarity with command line basics

data.title: Wireshark Certified Network Analyst
data.description: WCNA Certification (formerly referred to as the Wireshark Certified Network Analyst program) proves an individual's in-depth knowledge of TCP/IP and network/protocol analysis for troubleshooting communications, network optimization, network forensics, and security.

requirements_data.knowledge: Understanding of TCP/IP and basic networking concepts

data.title: Certified Master Cyber Threat Analyst
data.description: Individuals who pass all four certification exams C)VA, C)PEH, C)TIA and C)CSA will earn the Master Cyber Threat Analyst Certification. Persons with this certification have demonstrated the knowledge needed to proactively monitor, detect, prevent, and mitigate threats as they arise in real time. They have also shown that they can set up and deploy analysis tools, intrusion detection tools, syslog servers, SIEMs to find and in many cases, prevent, exploits. They will have demonstrated an ability to detect cyber attacks and deploy blue team tactics to help prevent future attacks. Plus an ability to effectively communicate suggestions and procedures.

data.title: The Certified SOC Analyst
data.description: CSA validates the skills required to work in a Security Operations Center (SOC). It assesses proficiency in SOC operations, log management, SIEM deployment, incident detection, and response.

requirements_data.knowledge: Basic understanding of networking and security concepts
requirements_data.work_experience: one year of work experience in the Network Admin/Security domain (waived if an official EC-Council training course is completed)

data.title: Kali Linux Certified Professional
data.description: KLCP validates proficiency with the Kali Linux distribution, focusing on managing, customizing, and securing the operating system for penetration testing. It assesses skills in package management, system configuration, and creating custom Kali Linux environments.
domains_covered_titles: Configuring, Securing, and Monitoring Kali Linux

requirements_data.knowledge: Basic Linux proficiency and understanding of Kali Linux

data.title: Certified Red Team Analyst
data.description: The Certified Red Team Analyst (CRTA) certification validates foundational skills in red teaming and offensive security, focusing on Active Directory exploitation, post-exploitation, and lateral movement. CRTA-certified professionals demonstrate the ability to simulate real-world adversary tactics, techniques, and procedures (TTPs) to enhance organizational security posture.

requirements_data.knowledge: Basic familiarity with penetration testing and command-line usage (Linux/Windows)

data.title: Security Operations Analyst Associate
data.description: Professional certification for Security Operations Analysts who investigate, search for, and mitigate threats using Microsoft Sentinel, Microsoft Defender for Cloud, Microsoft 365 Defender, and third-party security products. Validates skills in threat hunting, incident response, and security operations.

requirements_data.knowledge: Familiarity with Microsoft 365, Azure cloud services, Windows, Linux, and mobile operating systems; proficiency in Kusto Query Language (KQL) and Microsoft security tools.
requirements_data.work_experience: 1-2 years in security operations or related roles recommended.

exam_details_data.format: Proctored, multiple-choice, and scenario-based questions with interactive components
